---
title: "Sergio Ibarra-Espinosa"
image: "avatar.jpeg"
links:
  - LinkedIn: "https://linkedin.com/sibarra"
  - Twitter: "https://twitter.com/SergioIbarraEs1"
  - GitHub: "https://github.com/ibarraespinosa"
  - Email: "mailto:sergio.ibarra@usp.br"
  - Researchgate: "https://www.researchgate.net/profile/Sergio_Ibarra-Espinosa"
  - ORCID: "https://orcid.org/0000-0002-3162-1905"
  - CV: "http://buscatextual.cnpq.br/buscatextual/visualizacv.do?metodo=apresentar&id=K4695619Y4"
output:
  postcards::jolla_blue
---

## Bio

I study vehicular emissions and its impact on air quality. I develop R packages with Fortran
interfaces make the calculations faster. I have worked with big data of GPS IoT to
develop traffic flow. I developed the model VEIN to estimate vehicular emission.
VEIN is used in many countries in the globe and includes emission factors for China,
Europe, Brazil and United States. I collaborate with researches in air quality, mobility
and climate change.

## Education

**Universidade de São Paulo** | São Paulo, Brazil

Ph.D. in Atmospheric Science | July 2013 - October 2017

**Universidad de Chile** | Santiago, Chile

MSc. in Environmental Management| March 2010 - May December 2011
